turns.content: Well, I've had this cough for about two weeks now. It started off kind of mild, but it just won't go away. And something else that's really worrying me is that when I walk up the stairs at school, I sometimes feel like I can't catch my breath. That's never happened to me before.

The cough started about two weeks ago, right after a bad cold I had. As for the breathlessness, I think that started maybe five or six days ago—it's hard to say exactly, but it's been getting more noticeable.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

I wouldn't say it's sharp pain, more of a tight, heavy feeling right in the center of my chest. It's not constant—it mostly happens when I'm active or when I have a bad coughing spell. It's almost like someone's pressing down on me from inside, if that makes any sense.
Have you had any fever or chills?

I haven't had a fever that I've measured, but I did feel a little flushed and clammy one evening last week. No real chills, though. Mostly it's just the cough and that heavy chest feeling that bother me.
What medications are you currently taking?

I'm not on anything regularly, just an over-the-counter allergy pill sometimes when the seasons change. I do have an old inhaler from a few years ago, but I haven't needed to use it until this past week—I tried it once or twice for the breathlessness, but it didn't seem to help much.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No, I don't have any chronic conditions that I'm aware of. I've always been pretty healthy, just occasional colds and seasonal allergies.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, I'm not allergic to any medications that I know of. I've taken things like ibuprofen and antibiotics before without any problems.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

I've been drinking lots of warm tea with honey, and I've been trying to rest more, but it doesn't seem to make a difference. I also took that old inhaler I mentioned a couple of times, but it barely touched the tightness, so I stopped using it.
